Title: MsgPulse IPC
Description: The system shall facilitate IPC via QNX MsgPulse for asynchronous watchdog heartbeats and event notification.

Title: Core Affinity - WDG
Description: The Watchdog (Consumer) process shall be bound to CPU Core 0 to ensure high-availability monitoring.

Title: Core Affinity - Producer
Description: The Producer process shall be bound to CPU Core 2 to isolate high-frequency generation load.

Title: Windowed Watchdog Logic
Description: The system shall implement a Windowed (PWM-style) Watchdog. Total Cycle: 400ms. Open Window: 0-300ms (Pulses accepted). Closed Window: 300-400ms (Pulses rejected as logic failure).

Title: Periodic Heartbeat
Description: The Producer shall transmit a MsgPulse heartbeat every 200ms (center of the 300ms open window) using CLOCK_MONOTONIC to ensure delivery despite scheduling jitter.

Title: Synchronous Handshake
Description: The Producer shall establish a connection via name_open() and perform a synchronous MsgSend/MsgReply handshake with the Watchdog before starting heartbeats.

Title: Initialization Handshake
Description: The Producer shall wait for handshake with the software watchdog for 10s before failing.

Title: Pulse Reception
Description: The Watchdog shall use MsgReceivePulse() to block on the channel and process incoming heartbeats with minimal latency.

Title: Watchdog Failure Handling
Description: The Watchdog shall maintain a failure counter for missed pulses or pulses arriving in the closed window. Upon 5 consecutive failures
Priority: the system shall transition to a Safe State (e.g. Hardware Reset).

Title: Channel Management
Description: The Watchdog shall create the QNX Channel (ChannelCreate) and register its name via name_attach(). Channel priority shall be set to 99.

Title: Warm Reconnection
Description: The Watchdog shall support Producer reconnection to the existing Channel without requiring a full system restart.

Title: Deadlock Avoidance
Description: The system shall employ a single-lock strategy.

Title: Resource Validation
Description: Both processes shall validate channel creation and exit with non-zero status on failure.

Title: Signal Safety
Description: Signal handlers shall be limited to async-signal-safe operations (e.g. broadcasting cond var).

Title: E2E Pulse Protection
Description: The 32-bit MsgPulse payload shall include an 8-bit Sequence Counter and an 8-bit CRC to ensure data integrity and detect stale pulses (ASIL B).

Title: Temporal Isolation
Description: Producer and Consumer timing shall be driven by CLOCK_MONOTONIC and remain independent to prevent timing-related cascaded failures and clock-jump sensitivity.

Title: FIFO Integrity
Description: Verify that messages are dequeued in the exact order they were enqueued under high load.

Title: Window Violation Detection
Description: Verify that the Watchdog triggers a failure if a pulse is received between 300ms and 400ms of the cycle.

Title: Channel Persistence
Description: Verify that the QNX Channel remains available if the Producer process is killed and restarted.
